blurb: The Secure Enclave is a dedicated security coprocessor embedded in Apple SoCs, running sepOS – an Apple-customized L4 microkernel. It has its own encrypted memory, hardware TRNG, AES engine, and PKA, and communicates with the application processor only through a tightly restricted interrupt-driven mailbox. The Secure Enclave manages biometric template processing, wraps Data Protection class keys with device UID-derived material, and performs Sealed Key Protection (SKP) to bind encryption keys to measurements of system software. No code outside the Secure Enclave can access its raw private key material.
